title: Atlas Backlog
description: Data contract for a project backlog consumed by the Atlas Plugin

modules.description: Registry of module names referenced by stories[].module and tasks[].module. Every distinct value used in stories[].module or tasks[].module SHOULD appear here as a `name` (referential integrity is enforced by the compliance workflow on backlog-change PRs, not by JSON Schema alone — pure JSON Schema can't cross-reference arrays). When introducing a new module name via /atlas-update, add it to this registry in the same PR.

id.description: Stable module identifier (e.g. M1, M2). Reused across renames.

name.description: Human-readable module name shown in PhaseCards, drill-down, and chip labels. This is what stories[].module references.

kb_articles.description: Slugs of KB articles supporting this story. Bidirectional with kb/_index.json#/articles[].related_stories.

estimate_ai_hours.description: Estimated hours of AI-agent time required to complete this story end-to-end (claim → merge). Set on add / prd-intake / refine. Used by Telemetry to plot estimate-vs-actual + roll up phase-level burn-down. Reconciler captures the actual via merged_at - first_event_at and surfaces the delta in DrillDownPanel. v1.8.1+.

actual_ai_hours.description: Reconciler-populated actual AI-hours from claim → merge. Read-only via /atlas-update; the reconciler is the only writer. v1.8.1+.

supersedes.description: ID of the story this one replaces. When set, the predecessor's `superseded_by` should include this story.

superseded_by.description: IDs of stories that replace this one (used for splits and rewrites). When non-empty, this story is retired — reconciler ignores it for PR matching + KPI math.

merges_from.description: IDs of stories that were merged INTO this one. The listed predecessors should be marked superseded_by: [<this id>].

deferred_reason.description: Why this story was deferred. Required when state === 'deferred'. Free text — shown in the drill-down.

depends_on.description: IDs of stories/tasks that must reach state merged or verified before this story may be claimed.

ui.description: True if this is a visual/UI change; routes the story to in_review for human QA sign-off and blocks verified until signed off.

worktree_branch.description: The feat/<id>-… branch this story is claimed on, or null.

evolution_log.description: Append-only audit trail of /atlas-update operations on this story. Each entry captures who, when, why, and what changed. Surfaced in the DrillDownPanel + Activity feed + Changes page so Delivery Managers can audit plan changes.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 of when the change was applied (PR merge time).

operation.description: Which /atlas-update operation produced this entry. prd-intake = a story landed as part of a bulk PRD ingest (the same reason text typically appears on every story in the batch). phase-create / phase-update = the entry was written because a brand-new phase was added (or its metadata changed) in atlas.config.json under the same PR; the story field deltas live in `changes` but the structural shift is at phase-overlay level.

by.description: GitHub login of the dev/agent that opened the backlog-change PR.

reason.description: Required justification — why this change is being made. DM-visible.

changes.description: Optional structured diff. Free-form per-field — e.g. { acceptance: '3 items → 4 items', phase: '3 → 4' }.

pr_url.description: URL of the atlas:backlog-change PR that introduced the change.

story_id.description: Parent story ID (links into stories[].id). Required when stories[] is present.

ui.description: True if this is a visual/UI change requiring human QA sign-off.

blocker.description: Orthogonal flag — task is blocked by an external factor or human decision, INDEPENDENT of its lifecycle state. A claimed task can be blockered (waiting on legal review), a pr_open task can be blockered (waiting on a vendor), etc. Cleared by setting to null. v1.8.2+.

reason.description: Human-readable reason — what is blocking, and what would unblock it.

since.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the blocker was set.

by.description: GitHub login of who flagged the blocker.

needs_human_review.description: Orthogonal flag — AI implementer believes the work is complete but a specific human's eyes are required before merge. Layers on top of lifecycle state. Cleared by setting to null. v1.8.2+.

reviewer.description: GitHub login of the requested reviewer (optional — falls back to any human reviewer on the PR).

context.description: Why human review is needed for THIS task. E.g. 'AI auto-merged a similar pattern incorrectly last week; please verify the rate-limit math'.

since.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when human review was requested.

by.description: GitHub login of who requested review.
